{{Short description|none}}
This is a list of '''inventions and discoveries by Israeli scientists and researchers,''' working locally or overseas.
==Mathematics==
* [[Johnson–Lindenstrauss lemma]], a mathematical result concerning low-distortion embeddings of points from high-dimensional into low-dimensional Euclidean space contributed by [[Joram Lindenstrauss]].
* Development of the measurement of rigidity by [[Elon Lindenstrauss]] in [[ergodic theory]], and their applications to [[number theory]].<ref>{{cite web| title = Fields Medal – Elon Lindenstrauss, ICM2010 | url = http://www.icm2010.in/imu-prizes/prize-winners-2010/fields-medal-elon-lindenstrauss}}</ref>
* A proof of [[Szemerédi's theorem]] using ergodic theory, by mathematician [[Hillel Furstenberg]].
* Expansion of [[axiomatic set theory]] and the [[Zermelo–Fraenkel axioms|ZF set theory]] by [[Abraham Fraenkel]].
* Development of the area of [[automorphic form]]s and [[L-functions]] by [[Ilya Piatetski-Shapiro]].<ref>{{Citation | title=Ilya Piatetski-Shapiro, In Memoriam | url=https://www.ams.org/notices/201010/rtx101001260p.pdf | year=2010 | journal=[[Notices of the American Mathematical Society]] | volume=57 | issue=10 | pages=1260–1275}}</ref><ref>[http://www.math.tau.ac.il/people/memoriam/Piatetski-Shapiro-eng.pdf Tel Aviv University obituary] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091229191506/http://www.math.tau.ac.il/people/memoriam/Piatetski-Shapiro-eng.pdf |date=2009-12-29 }}</ref>
* Development of [[Sauer–Shelah lemma]] and [[Shelah cardinal]].
* Development of the first proof of the [[alternating sign matrix conjecture]].
* Development of [[Zig-zag product of graphs]], a method of combining smaller graphs to produce larger ones used in the construction of expander graphs by [[Avi Wigderson]]. 
* Development of [[Bernstein–Sato polynomial]] and proof of the [[Kazhdan–Lusztig conjectures]] by [[Joseph Bernstein]]
* Generalization of the [[Hall's marriage theorem|marriage theorem]] by obtaining the right transfinite conditions for infinite bipartite graphs. He subsequently proved the appropriate versions of the [[Kőnig's theorem (graph theory)|Kőnig theorem]] and the [[Menger theorem]] for infinite graphs by [[Ron Aharoni]].
* Development of the [[Amitsur–Levitzki theorem]] by [[Shimshon Amitsur]].
* The Benjamini-Hochberg procedure for controlling the [[False discovery rate]], a statistical method for regulating [[Type I error]]s.<ref name=BenjaminiHochberg1995>{{cite journal | vauthors = Benjamini Y, Hochberg Y | year = 1995 | title = Controlling the false discovery rate: a practical and powerful approach to multiple testing | journal = [[Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B]] | volume = 57 | issue = 1 | pages = 289–300 | doi = 10.1111/j.2517-6161.1995.tb02031.x | mr = 1325392  | url = http://www.math.tau.ac.il/~ybenja/MyPapers/benjamini_hochberg1995.pdf}}</ref>

== Science ==
=== Chemistry ===
* Discovery of [[quasicrystal]]s by [[Dan Shechtman]] of the Technion.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://physicsworld.com/cws/article/news/3106|title=Quasicrystals clinch Wolf prize|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref> The discovery led him to receive the 2011 [[Nobel Prize in Chemistry]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/chemistry/laureates/2011/shechtman-facts.html|title=Dan Shechtman - Facts|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* Discovery of the role of protein [[Ubiquitin]] by [[Avram Hershko]] and [[Aaron Ciechanover]] of the [[Technion – Israel Institute of Technology|Technion]] Institute (together with the American biologist [[Irwin Rose]]). The discovery led them to receive the 2004 [[Nobel Prize in Chemistry]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.israel21c.org/health/two-israelis-win-nobel-prize-in-chemistry|title=Two Israelis win Nobel Prize in chemistry|work=ISRAEL21c|date=10 October 2004|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/chemistry/laureates/2004/|title=The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2004|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* Increased understanding of how proteins are made - [[Ada Yonath]] of Israel alongside [[Venkatraman Ramakrishnan]] of India and [[Thomas A. Steitz]] of the US shared the 2009 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for increased understanding of the structure and function of [[ribosomes]].<ref>[https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/chemistry/2009/prize-announcement Nobel Prizew in Chemistry 2009]/</ref>
* Development of multiscale models for complex chemical systems by [[Arieh Warshel]] and [[Michael Levitt (biophysicist)|Michael Levitt]] of the [[Weizmann Institute of Science]] (presently at [[University of Southern California]] and [[Stanford University]], respectively), together with the Austrian-born American chemist [[Martin Karplus]]. The discovery led them to receive the 2013 [[Nobel Prize in Chemistry]].<ref>[https://www.nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/chemistry/laureates/2013/press.pdf The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2013]</ref>
* Discovery and Isolation of the [[Tetrahydrocannabinol]] (THC), a [[cannabinoid]] found in [[Cannabis (drug)|cannabis]], by [[Raphael Mechoulam]] while working in the [[Weizmann Institute of Science]], 1964.<ref name="Williams">{{cite web |author-last=Williams |author-first=Alex |date=March 22, 2023 |title=Raphael Mechoulam, 'Father of Cannabis Research,' Dies at 92 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/03/22/science/raphael-mechoulam-dead.html |website=[[The New York Times]]}}</ref><ref name="Maccarrone">{{cite journal |last1=Maccarrone |first1=M |date=5 January 2022 |title=Tribute to Professor Raphael Mechoulam, The Founder of Cannabinoid and Endocannabinoid Research. |journal=Molecules (Basel, Switzerland) |volume=27 |issue=1 |page=323 |doi=10.3390/molecules27010323 |pmc=8746417 |pmid=35011553 |doi-access=free}}</ref><ref name="Mechoulam">{{Cite journal |last=Mechoulam |first=Raphael |date=2023-01-20 |title=A Delightful Trip Along the Pathway of Cannabinoid and Endocannabinoid Chemistry and Pharmacology |journal=Annual Review of Pharmacology and Toxicology |language=en |volume=63 |issue=1 |pages=1–13 |doi=10.1146/annurev-pharmtox-051921-083709 |issn=0362-1642 |pmid=35850522 |s2cid=250642163 |doi-access=free}}</ref>

=== Physics ===
{{see also|Category:Israeli physicists}}
* Discovery of the [[Aharonov–Bohm effect]] by [[Yakir Aharonov]] and [[David Bohm]].<ref>[http://www.perimeterinstitute.ca/News/In_The_Media/PI_Distinguished_Research_Chair_Yakir_Aharonov_Wins_US_National_Medal_of_Science/ PI Distinguished Research Chair Yakir Aharonov Wins US National Medal of Science - Perimeter Institute for Theoretical Physics] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110524084043/http://www.perimeterinstitute.ca/News/In_The_Media/PI_Distinguished_Research_Chair_Yakir_Aharonov_Wins_US_National_Medal_of_Science/ |date=2011-05-24 }}</ref>
* [[Jacob Bekenstein]] was the first to propose [[Black hole]]s also have [[Entropy]] while working in the [[Hebrew University of Jerusalem]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.3rd1000.com/chronology/chrono15.htm|title=Chronology of Black Hole Physics|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://physics.ie/resources/physics-timeline/physics-timeline-1961-1980/|title=Physics.ie - Online Physics Courses - Physics Timeline 1961 – 1980|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* Discovery and classification of [[Hadron|hadrons]] through the [[SU(3)]] [[flavour symmetry]], led to the development of [[Eightfold way (physics)|Eightfold way]], by [[Yuval Ne'eman]], 1961.<ref>{{cite web |author=Basch_Interactive |date=1980-01-01 |title=Presidents of Tel Aviv University &#124; Tel Aviv University &#124; Tel Aviv University |url=https://english.tau.ac.il/list_of_presidents |access-date=2020-02-11 |publisher=English.tau.ac.il}}</ref><ref>Yuval Ne'eman Dies at 80 – Watson 2006 (426): 1. ScienceNOW</ref>

=== Optics ===
[[File:CapsuleEndoscope.jpg|thumb|Endoscopic capsule]]
* [[Pillcam]] by [[Given Imaging]], the first [[Capsule endoscopy|Capsule Endoscopy]] solution to record images of the [[digestive tract]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-05-24|title=Q&A: Medtronic's latest gastrointestinal offering|url=https://www.med-technews.com/api/content/040b5984-b886-11eb-b75f-1244d5f7c7c6/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Med-Tech Innovation {{!}} Latest news for the medical device industry|language=en-gb}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Pill camera offered as an alternative to colonoscopy|url=https://www.mdlinx.com/article/pill-camera-offered-as-an-alternative-to-colonoscopy/lfc-832|access-date=2021-06-02|website=MDLinx}}</ref> The capsule is the size and shape of a pill and contains a tiny camera.<ref>[http://www.israel21c.org/health/israel-s-camera-in-a-capsule-approved-for-esophagus-diagnosis Israel's 'camera in a capsule' approved for esophagus diagnosis] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20120221163758/http://www.israel21c.org/health/israel-s-camera-in-a-capsule-approved-for-esophagus-diagnosis |date=2012-02-21 }}</ref> Created by Israeli engineer [[Gavriel Iddan]]<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-05-27|title=20 Years of PillCam Capsule Endoscopy Innovation|url=https://www.mddionline.com/design-engineering/20-years-pillcam-capsule-endoscopy-innovation|access-date=2021-06-02|website=mddionline.com|language=en}}</ref> who sold the company to Irish medical device maker Covidien for $860 million.<ref>{{Cite news|website=Reuters |title=Covidien buys camera-in-a-pill maker Given Imaging for $860 mln|language=en-US|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/givenimaging-covidien-idUSL6N0JN03I20131208|access-date=2021-06-02}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2013-12-08|title=Covidien Announces Definitive Agreement to Acquire Given Imaging|url=https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20131207005048/en/Covidien-Announces-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Given-Imaging|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.businesswire.com|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2012-11-30|title=Given Imaging to Be Acquired for $860 Million|url=https://www.globalatlanta.com/given-imaging-to-be-acquired-for-860-million/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Global Atlanta|language=en-US}}</ref> Iddan has expressed regret for the sale due to the companies fulfillment of an ancient Jewish prophecy “''The Pillcam was based on military technology... It was a good example of how we shall beat our swords into plowshares''", as the Hebrew prophets predicted.<ref>{{cite news|last=Shamah|first=David|title=Pillcam's inventor regrets sale of 'Biblical' tech to foreign firm|url=http://www.timesofisrael.com/pillcams-inventor-regrets-sale-of-biblical-tech-to-foreign-firm/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=[[The Times of Israel]]|date=23 April 2015 |language=en-US |issn=0040-7909}}</ref> Covidien was acquired by Medtronic in 2016, and is now the provider of Pillcam.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Analyzing Medtronic's Acquisition of Covidien|url=http://finance.yahoo.com/news/analyzing-medtronic-acquisition-covidien-080445888.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=finance.yahoo.com|date=15 April 2016 |language=en-US}}</ref>
* Line free single power bicentric prismatic spectacle lens for correction of anisometropia. Sydney J. Bush UK patent no. 1539381.

=== Medicine ===
* The flexible [[stent]], also known as NIR Stent or EluNIR. Developed by Israeli company Medinol, which is headquartered in Tel Aviv.<ref>{{Cite web|title=NIR Legacy of Innovation|url=https://www.medinol.com/nir-legacy-innovation/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Medinol|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Boston Scientific Gets FDA Approval for the NIR Coronary Stent|url=https://www.meddeviceonline.com/doc/boston-scientific-gets-fda-approval-for-the-n-0001|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.meddeviceonline.com}}</ref>
*The [[Emergency Bandage|pressure bandage]] - known widely as the [[Israeli bandage|Israeli Bandage]] is a specially designed, first-aid device that is used to stop [[bleeding]] from [[hemorrhagic]] wounds caused by traumatic injuries in pre-hospital emergency situations.<ref>{{Cite web|last=bryanpblack|date=2011-10-19|title=Why Israeli Bandages are like Pepperidge Farm Bread|url=https://www.itstactical.com/medcom/medical/why-israeli-bandages-are-like-pepperidge-farm-bread/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=ITS Tactical|language=en-US}}</ref> First used for saving lives during a [[SFOR|NATO peacekeeping operation]] in [[Bosnia and Herzegovina]],<ref>{{Cite web|last=admin|date=2020-02-13|title=Israeli Bandage: Uses and Basic Guide|url=https://explorefirstaid.com/israeli-bandage-uses-and-basic-guide/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Explore First Aid|language=en-US}}</ref> by inventor, [[Israel Defense Forces|Israeli military]] medic, Bernard Bar-Natan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Editor's Notes: The guy with the bandage|url=https://www.jpost.com/opinion/columnists/editors-notes-the-guy-with-the-bandage|access-date=2021-06-02|website=[[The Jerusalem Post]]|date=29 April 2011 |language=en-US |issn=0792-822X}}</ref> The bandage was successfully used during operations ''[[Enduring Freedom]]'' and ''[[Iraqi Freedom]]'' and is widely used today, across the world.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Blue Line Magazine cover story - Feb 2014 by David A. Brown|url=http://www.bulletproofset.ca/page8/page10/page10.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.bulletproofset.ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-06-02|title=Trauma kits used by police officers help save lives during recent events in Indianapolis|url=https://fox59.com/news/trauma-kits-for-shooting-victims/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Fox 59|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last1=Kramer|first1=Linda G.|last2=Berea|first2=City of|date=2021-05-05|title=American Legion donates trauma packs to Berea police: Community Voices|url=https://www.cleveland.com/community/2021/05/american-legion-donates-trauma-packs-to-berea-police-community-voices.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=cleveland|language=en}}</ref> The bandage was nicknamed "Israeli bandage" by [[United States|American]] soldiers, and has been "the bandage of choice for the US Army and special forces". Before the Israeli emergency bandage was invented in 1998, wounded soldiers were told to find a rock and wrap it on top of hemorrhaging wounds in order to hold direct pressure.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-25|title=How to Use an Israeli Bandage to Stop Bleeding|url=https://insidefirstaid.com/bleeding/what-is-and-how-to-use-israeli-bandage|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Inside First Aid|language=en-us}}</ref> Bar-Natan sold his company to PerSys Medical Inc in Houston, Texas, the company that first introduced the bandage to the US military.[[File:A Sudanese service member, right, applies a bandage to U.S. Navy Petty Officer 3rd Class Douglas Barton's arm during exercise Cutlass Express 2013 at the Port of Djibouti in Djibouti Nov. 13, 2013 131113-F-NJ596-036.jpg|alt=A Sudanese service member, right, applies a bandage to U.S. Navy Petty Officer 3rd Class Douglas Barton's arm during exercise Cutlass Express 2013 at the Port of Djibouti in Djibouti Nov. 13, 2013|thumb|An Israeli Bandage being used by Sudanese and US Naval service members during a training exercise.]]
*[[Eshkol-Wachman Movement Notation]] – a notation system for recording movement on paper that has been used in many fields, including [[dance]], [[physical therapy]], animal behavior and early diagnosis of [[autism]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.haaretz.com/print-edition/news/co-founder-of-movement-notation-system-dies-at-79-1.320982|title=Co-founder of movement notation system dies at 79|author=Elad Samorzik|date=25 October 2010|work=Haaretz.com|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* Development of the [[Glatiramer acetate|Copaxone]] [[immunomodulator]] drug for treating [[multiple sclerosis]]. It was developed in the [[Weizmann Institute of Science]] in Israel by [[Michael Sela]], [[Ruth Arnon]] and Deborah Teitelbaum.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://people.forbes.com/profile/ruth-arnon/22228|title=Sheikha Mozah Bint Nasser Al-Missned|author=Zina Moukheiber|work=Forbes|access-date=10 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110611090207/http://people.forbes.com/profile/ruth-arnon/22228|archive-date=11 June 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://globalhealthvision.wordpress.com/category/multiple-sclerosis/|title=Multiple Sclerosis « Global Health Vision|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* Development of bioengineered [[wikt:recombinant|recombinant]] [[interferon]] [[protein]]s by Michel Revel from the Weizmann Institute of Science in Israel.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://thefutureofthings.com/3540-interferon-based-drug-may-eliminate-breast-cancer/|last=Rotman|first=Einat|title=Interferon Based Drug May Eliminate Breast Cancer|year=2022|access-date=17 October 2022|website=The Future of Things}}</ref>
* Development of [[taliglucerase alfa]] (Elelyso), a recombinant [[glucocerebrosidase]] enzyme produced from [[Genetically modified plant|transgenic]] carrot cell cultures.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=biGwUBUpTZMC&q=protalix&pg=PA60|title=Molecular Farming in Plants: Recent Advances and Future Prospects|first=Aiming |last=Wang|page=60|isbn=978-94-007-2216-3|year=2011|publisher=[[Springer Science+Business Media|Springer]]}}</ref> Taliglucerase alfa won approval from the [[U.S. Food and Drug Administration]] in May 2012 as an [[orphan drug]] for the treatment of Type 1 [[Gaucher's disease]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Yukhananov|first=Anna|title=U.S. FDA approves Pfizer/Protalix drug for Gaucher|url=http://www.chicagotribune.com/health/sns-rt-us-fda-gaucherbre8401jz-20120501,0,5155428.story|access-date=2 May 2012|newspaper=[[Chicago Tribune]]|date=1 May 2012|agency=[[Reuters]]}} {{dead link|date=December 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>
*Development of ENvue, a feeding tube placement system with advanced methods of navigation, integrated sensors and body mapping, for accurate enteral tube placement, by the Israeli company ENvizion Medical, used in Hospitals and Medical centers in the US.<ref>{{Cite news |last= |first= |date=2022-11-22 |title=Envizion unveils baby feeding tube with navigation |url=https://en.globes.co.il/en/article-envizion-unveils-baby-feeding-tube-with-navigation-1001387665 |access-date=2022-12-05 |website=Globes |language=en-US}}</ref>
* Development of engineered recombinant [[Chimeric Antigen Receptor]]s by Professor [[Zelig Eshhar]] of the Weizmann Institute of Science and his trainee [[Gideon Gross]], today a component of novel cell-based [[immunotherapy]] for various cancers.<ref>{{cite journal |journal=Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America|title=Expression of immunoglobulin-T-cell receptor chimeric molecules a functional receptors with antibody-type specificity|year=1989|volume=86|issue=24|pages=10024–10028|doi=10.1073/pnas.86.24.10024|doi-access=free|pmid=2513569|pmc=298636|last1=Gross|first1=G|last2=Waks|first2=T|last3=Eshhar|first3=Z|bibcode=1989PNAS...8610024G }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |website=Dan David Prize|title=Prof. Zelig Eshhar|year=2021|access-date=17 October 2022|url=https://dandavidprize.org/laureates/prof-zelig-eshhar/}}</ref>
* Development of [[Azilect]], a drug for [[Parkinson's disease]], by Moussa Youdim and John Finberg from the Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, and commercialized by [[Teva Pharmaceutical Industries]].

=== Economics ===
* Work of [[Daniel Kahneman]] and [[Amos Tversky]] of the Hebrew University of Jerusalem explaining irrational human economic choices.<ref>{{cite journal | last1 = Tversky | first1 = Amos | last2 = Kahneman | first2 = Daniel | year = 1981 | title = The Framing of Decisions and the Psychology of Choice | journal = Science | volume = 211 | issue = 4481| pages = 453–458 | doi=10.1126/science.7455683 | pmid=7455683| bibcode = 1981Sci...211..453T | s2cid = 5643902 }}</ref> The work led Daniel to receive the 2002 [[Nobel Prize in Economics]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/economic-sciences/laureates/2002/|title=The Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel 2002|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref> 
* Developments in [[Game theory]]. [[Israel Aumann]] of the Hebrew University of Jerusalem received the 2005 [[Nobel Prize in Economics]] for his work in this field.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/economics/laureates/2005/aumann-lecture.html|title=Robert J. Aumann - Prize Lecture: War and Peace|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* The [[Rubinstein bargaining model]], one of the most influential findings in [[game theory]], refers to a class of bargaining games that feature alternating offers through an infinite time horizon. The proof is from [[Ariel Rubinstein]] 1982.<ref name=Rubinstein1982>{{cite journal
 | author = Rubinstein, Ariel
 | year = 1982
 | title = Perfect Equilibrium in a Bargaining Model
 | journal = Econometrica
 | volume = 50
 | issue = 1
 | pages = 97–109
 | doi = 10.2307/1912531
 | url = http://arielrubinstein.tau.ac.il/papers/11.pdf
| jstor = 1912531
 | citeseerx = 10.1.1.295.1434
 | s2cid = 14827857
 }}</ref>

=== Biotechnology ===
{{see also|Category:Israeli biologists}}
[[File:Eshkol-Wachman Movement Notation SoR example 3.gif|thumb|150px|Eshkol-Wachman Movement Notation]]
* [[Nanowire]] – a [[Electrical conductor|conductive wire]] made of a string of tiny particles of silver, a thousand times thinner than a [[human hair]]. Developed by [[Uri Sivan]], Erez Braun and Yoav Eichen from [[Technion – Israel Institute of Technology|Technion]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/1998-02/ASfT-DUTC-180298.php|title=DNA Used To Create Self Assembling Conducting Wire: Breakthrough Will Lead To Next Leap In Emerging Nanoelectronics|date=18 February 1998|work=EurekAlert!|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[DNA computing|World's smallest DNA computing machine system]] – "the smallest biological computing device" ever constructed, according to [[Guinness Book of Records]], which is composed of enzymes and DNA molecules capable of performing simple mathematical calculations and which uses its input DNA molecule as its sole source of energy. Developed in 2003 in the [[Weizmann Institute of Science]] by professor [[Ehud Shapiro]] and his team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2003/02/0224_030224_DNAcomputer.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20030226202101/http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2003/02/0224_030224_DNAcomputer.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=February 26, 2003|title=Computer Made from DNA and Enzymes|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2003/02/030227074409.htm|title=Tiny Computing Machine Fueled By DNA; Device Awarded In Guinness World Record For "Smallest Biological Computing Device"|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[OrCam device|OrCam MyEye]], is a portable, artificial vision device that allows the visually impaired to understand text and identify objects through audio feedback describing what such people are unable to see.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.orcam.com/media/israeli-invention-gives-blind-a-way-to-see-faces/|title=Israeli Invention Gives Blind a Way to 'See' Faces - OrCam - See For Yourself|work=OrCam - See For Yourself|access-date=2017-08-13|language=en-US}}</ref>
* TraitUP – a new technology that enables the introduction of genetic materials into seeds without modifying their DNA, immediately and efficiently improving plants before they're even sowed. It was developed by Hebrew University agricultural scientists Ilan Sela and Haim D. Rabinowitch.<ref name=israel-feeds-world>{{cite news|author=Abigail Klein Leichman|title=The top 12 ways Israel feeds the world|url=http://israel21c.org/technology/the-top-12-ways-israel-feeds-the-world/|access-date=6 November 2013|newspaper=ISRAEL21c|date=May 10, 2012}}</ref>

=== Theoretical computer science ===
*[[RSA (cryptosystem)|RSA]] public key encryption, introduced by [[Adi Shamir]] with Ron Rivest, and Leonard Adleman<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://amturing.acm.org/award_winners/shamir_2327856.cfm|title = Adi Shamir - A.M. Turing Award Laureate}}</ref>
*The concept of [[nondeterministic finite automaton]]s, introduced by [[Michael O. Rabin]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://awards.acm.org/citation.cfm?id=9681074&srt=alpha&alpha=&aw=140&ao=AMTURING|title=Michael O. Rabin|access-date=10 July 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120714151843/http://awards.acm.org/citation.cfm?id=9681074&srt=alpha&alpha=&aw=140&ao=AMTURING|archive-date=14 July 2012}}</ref>
*[[Amir Pnueli]] introduced [[temporal logic]] into computing science
*[[Lempel–Ziv–Welch]] algorithm, a universal [[lossless data compression]] [[algorithm]] created by [[Abraham Lempel]] and [[Jacob Ziv]] of the Technion institute, together with the American Information theorist, [[Terry Welch]].<ref>[http://www.hpl.hp.com/about/honors/HPfellows/lempel.html HP Labs : About HP Labs : HP Fellows : Abraham Lempel] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120316072829/http://www.hpl.hp.com/about/honors/HPfellows/lempel.html |date=2012-03-16 }}</ref>
*[[Feige–Fiat–Shamir identification scheme]] - a type of parallel [[zero-knowledge proof]] developed by [[Uriel Feige]], [[Amos Fiat]], and [[Adi Shamir]] in 1988.

== Computing ==

*The [[Intel 8088]] – This [[microprocessor]], designed at [[Intel|Intel's]] [[Haifa]] laboratory, powered the first [[Personal computer|PC]] that [[IBM]] built,<ref>{{Cite web|title=7 Microprocessors That Powered the PC Revolution|url=https://www.pcmag.com/news/7-microprocessors-that-powered-the-pc-revolution|access-date=2021-06-02|website=PCMAG|date=17 March 2016 |language=en}}</ref> which is credited with kickstarting the [[Personal computer|PC]] revolution.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2003-01-23|title=IBM Archives: The birth of the IBM PC|url=https://www.ibm.com/ibm/history/exhibits/pc25/pc25_birth.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.ibm.com|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Derene|first=Glenn|date=2011-08-12|title=How IBM Sparked the Computer Revolution|url=https://www.popularmechanics.com/technology/gadgets/news/how-the-ibm-pc-sparked-a-computer-revolution|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Popular Mechanics|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=The computer which launched the PC revolution|url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/the-computer-which-launched-the-pc-revolution/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.cbsnews.com|date=11 August 2011 |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Greene|first=Jay|title=How IBM's 5150 PC shaped the computer industry|url=https://www.cnet.com/news/how-ibms-5150-pc-shaped-the-computer-industry/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=CNET|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=How IBM and the PC changed the world|url=https://www.computerweekly.com/news/2240059261/How-IBM-and-the-PC-changed-the-world|access-date=2021-06-02|website=ComputerWeekly.com|language=en}}</ref> The 8088 was designed in [[Israel]] at Intel's [[Haifa]] laboratory. The widespread use of the IBM's PC,<ref>{{Cite web|last=Engheim|first=Erik|date=2020-12-23|title=A world without the IBM PC|url=https://medium.com/lessons-from-history/a-world-without-the-ibm-pc-9db63c27b85e|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Medium|language=en}}</ref> using the 8088 processor, established the use of [[x86]] architecture as a de facto standard for decades. The [[Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers|IEEE]] wrote that "''almost all the world’s PCs are built around CPUs that can claim the 8088 as an ancesto''r."<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Chip Hall of Fame: Intel 8088 Microprocessor|url=https://spectrum.ieee.org/chip-hall-of-fame-intel-8088-microprocessor|access-date=2021-06-02|website=IEEE Spectrum: Technology, Engineering, and Science News|date=30 June 2017|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=The 8086 & 8088|url=http://cpumuseum.jimdofree.com/museum/intel/8086-8088/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=CPU Museum|language=en-US}}</ref> Intel has credited the 8088 with launching the company into the [[Fortune 500]].<ref name=":0" />
* [[Quicktionary]] [[Electronic dictionary]] – a pen-sized scanner able to scan words or phrases and immediately translate them into other languages, or keep them in memory in order to transfer them to the PC. Developed by the company [[Wizcom Technologies Ltd.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/finance/stocks/companyProfile?symbol=WZM.DE|title=Company Search Results|work=Reuters|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[Projection keyboard|Laser Keyboard]] – a virtual keyboard is projected onto a wall or table top and allows to type handheld computers and cell phones. Developed simultaneously by the Israeli company Lumio and Silicon Valley startup company Canesta.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.lumio.com/our-core-technology/virtual-keyboard.html|title=Virtual Keyboard|work=Lumio Inc.|access-date=10 July 2015|archive-date=28 September 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110928030252/http://www.lumio.com/our-core-technology/virtual-keyboard.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>Marriott, Michel (September 19, 2002). [https://query.nytimes.com/gst/fullpage.html?res=9C00E5D71230F93AA2575AC0A9649C8B63 "No Keys, Just Soft Light and You"]. ''The New York Times''.</ref><ref>Hesseldahl, Arik (September 18, 2002). [https://www.forbes.com/2002/09/18/0918tentech.html "Typing on the table"]. ''Forbes''.</ref><ref>Shiels, Maggie (October 15, 2002). [https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/in_depth/sci_tech/2000/dot_life/2326077.stm "The keyboard that isn't there"]. ''BBC News''.</ref> The company subsequently licensed the technology to Celluon of Korea.<ref name= cnetnews>Kanellos, Michael (September 19, 2006). [http://news.cnet.com/Honda-investing-in-chips-to-help-cars-see/2100-11389_3-6117385.html "Honda investing in chips to help cars see"]. ''CNET News''.</ref>
* Stateful Inspection as part of [[Firewall (computing)|firewalls]] - Although academic work on this had been performed by others, [[Gil Shwed|Gil Schwed]] and [[Nir Zuk]], at Israeli company [[Check Point|Checkpoint]], released the [[Check Point VPN-1|first commercial, stateful firewall]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Who Invented the Firewall?|url=https://www.darkreading.com/management/who-invented-the-firewall/208803808|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Dark Reading|date=15 January 2008|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=staff|first=CNET News|title=Check Point wins security patent|url=https://www.cnet.com/news/check-point-wins-security-patent/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=CNET|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Gil Shwed|url=https://www.forbes.com/profile/gil-shwed/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Forbes|language=en}}</ref>
* [[TDMoIP]] (TDM over IP) − in [[telecommunications]], the emulation of [[time-division multiplexing]] (TDM) over a [[packet-switched network]] (PSN), developed by engineers at [[RAD Data Communications]]<ref>{{cite web|title=Time Division Multiplexing over IP (TDMoIP)|date=December 2007 |doi=10.17487/RFC5087 |url=https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5087|last1=Shashoua |first1=R. |last2=Insler |first2=R. |last3=Anavi |first3=M. |last4=Anavi |first4=M. |doi-access=free}}</ref>
<!-- TODO: Replace above RFC ref with {{Ref RFC|5087|ref=yes}} once available at [[Template:Ref_RFC/db/50]] -->
* [[Voice over IP|Voice over Internet Protocol]] (VoIP) - technology for voice based communications using the internet instead of traditional telephone systems. VoIP was originally conceived by [[Danny Cohen (computer scientist)|Danny Cohen]], an Israeli-American scientist, but was first created, implemented, and commercialized by Netanya-based, Israeli company [[VocalTec]] and its founder [[Alon Cohen]]<ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-08-19|title=A Historical Journey into the VoIP Industry with Sigma Telecom|url=https://sigmatelecom.com/a-historical-journey-into-the-voip-industry-with-sigma-telecom/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Sigma Telecom {{!}} Global VoIP, SMS and Software Solutions Provider|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-08-31|title=History of VoiP Conversations{{!}} Cloud Vision Online|url=https://cloudvisiononline.com/the-history-of-voip/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Cloud Vision Technology|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Nexbridge :: IP Telephony – A Brief History of Its Technical Evolution|url=https://nexbridge.co.uk/ip-telephony-a-brief-history-of-its-technical-evolution/|access-date=2021-06-02|language=en-gb}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Voice over IP? {{!}} RingCentral|url=https://www.ringcentral.com/what-is-voice-over-ip.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.ringcentral.com|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=The History of VoIP and Internet Telephony (1928-Present) {{!}} GetVoIP|url=https://getvoip.com/blog/2014/01/27/history-of-voip-and-internet-telephones/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=getvoip.com|language=en-US}}</ref>
*[[Thunderbolt (interface)|Thunderbolt]], a widely used interface technology, was developed as a joint venture between Apple Inc and Intel, in Israel<ref>{{Cite news|date=2020-08-07|title=Intel's Thunderbolt 4, developed in Israel, enhances connectivity|language=en|work=Globes|url=https://en.globes.co.il/en/article-intels-thunderbolt-4-developed-in-israel-enhances-connectivity-1001335160|access-date=2021-06-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Shamah|first=David|title=Thunderbolt 3: How USB cooperation could lead to 100 million connected computers soon|url=https://www.zdnet.com/home-and-office/networking/thunderbolt-3-how-usb-cooperation-could-lead-to-100-million-connected-computers-soon/|access-date=2021-06-14|website=ZDNet|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Shah|first=Agam|date=2013-06-06|title=Intel shows 'world's fastest thumb drive'|url=https://www.computerworld.com/article/2711416/intel-shows--world-s-fastest-thumb-drive-.html|access-date=2021-06-14|website=Computerworld|language=en}}</ref>
*The first instant messaging (IM) service, [[ICQ]]. Originally developed by the [[State of Israel|Israeli]] company [[Mirabilis (company)|Mirabilis]] in 1996.  Mirabilis invented a system for creating unique identifiers (UINs) that could be applied over IRC leading it to be the first widely adopted IM service.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Petronzio|first=Matt|title=A Brief History of Instant Messaging|url=https://mashable.com/2012/10/25/instant-messaging-history/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Mashable|date=25 October 2012|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Moreau|first= Elise |title=10 Instant Message Tools That Once Dominated the Web|url=https://www.lifewire.com/once-popular-old-instant-messaging-services-3486135|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Lifewire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Desjardins|first=Jeff|date=2016-11-17|title=The Evolution of Instant Messaging|url=https://www.visualcapitalist.com/evolution-instant-messaging/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Visual Capitalist|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2001-03-28|title=How Instant Messaging Works|url=https://computer.howstuffworks.com/e-mail-messaging/instant-messaging.htm|access-date=2021-06-02|website=HowStuffWorks|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=A Brief History of Chat Apps · Guide to Chat Apps|url=https://towcenter.gitbooks.io/guide-to-chat-apps/content/introductionthe_dawn_of/a_brief_history.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=towcenter.gitbooks.io}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=ICQ with video calls, free messages and low-cost phone calls|url=http://www.icq.com/info/icqstory.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140225224016/http://www.icq.com/info/icqstory.html|archive-date=25 February 2014|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[Umoove]], a [[high-tech]] [[startup company]] that invented a [[software]] only solution for face and [[eye tracking]] is located in [[Israel]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mashable.com/2014/01/15/this-app-lets-you-control-a-game-using-face-tracking/|title=This App Tracks Your Face to Control a Game|author=Adario Strange|date=16 January 2014|work=Mashable|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[EPROM]] - a type of [[programmable read-only memory]] (PROM) [[Integrated circuit|chip]] that retains its data when its power supply is switched off. invented by [[Dov Frohman]] in 1971.<ref name="computerhistory1971">{{cite web |title=1971: Reusable semiconductor ROM introduced |url=https://www.computerhistory.org/storageengine/reusable-semiconductor-rom-introduced/ |access-date=19 June 2019 |website=[[Computer History Museum]]}}</ref> led to the development of [[Intel MCS-48]] and other [[Microcontroller|microcontrollers]].
*[[Network Vault]] - A system for secure data storage, exchange and/or sharing through a protected central storage facility, containing at least one "network vault" to which access is controlled through a single data access channel. developed by [[Alon Nisim Cohen]] and is used for Israeli software company [[CyberArk]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Globes and Dun & Bradstreet Israel Group 100 rankings of leading companies in the Israeli economy – Software companies |url=http://duns100.globes.co.il/en/rating?did=1001063718 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107103247/http://duns100.globes.co.il/en/rating?did=1001063718 |archive-date=2016-01-07 |access-date=2015-10-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-01-31 |title=United States Patent: 6356941 |url=http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-Parser?Sect1=PTO2&Sect2=HITOFF&p=1&u=/netahtml/PTO/search-bool.html&r=1&f=G&l=50&co1=AND&d=PTXT&s1=6356941.PN.&OS=PN/6356941&RS=PN/6356941 |access-date=2024-09-08 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180131023305/http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-Parser?Sect1=PTO2&Sect2=HITOFF&p=1&u=/netahtml/PTO/search-bool.html&r=1&f=G&l=50&co1=AND&d=PTXT&s1=6356941.PN.&OS=PN/6356941&RS=PN/6356941 |archive-date=2018-01-31 }}</ref>
*[[Face ID|FaceID]], the [[Apple Inc.|Apple Inc]] facial recognition software used in iPhone devices.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Will BDS supporters refrain from buying the iPhone X?|url=https://www.jpost.com/business-and-innovation/will-bds-supporters-refrain-from-buying-the-iphone-x-505155|access-date=2021-06-02|website=[[The Jerusalem Post]]|date=15 September 2017 |language=en-US |issn=0792-822X}}</ref> FaceID is based on technology created by Israeli companies [[PrimeSense]],<ref>{{Cite web|date=2017-10-15|title=iPhone X Face ID was developed in Israel - Israel Electronics News|url=https://techtime.news/2017/10/15/iphone-x/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Techtime News|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Apple's latest iPhone features new Israeli tech|url=https://www.fromthegrapevine.com/innovation/apple-event-new-iphone-11-face-id-features-tech-israel|access-date=2021-06-02|website=From the Grapevine|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Evans|first=Jonny|date=2017-09-13|title=iPhone X & Face ID: Everything you need to know|url=https://www.computerworld.com/article/3224569/iphone-x-and-face-id-everything-you-need-to-know.html|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Computerworld|language=en}}</ref> which was bought by [[Apple Inc.]] for $360 million on November 24, 2013,<ref>{{Cite web|last=Dormehl|first=Luke|date=2019-04-01|title=Apple drops plans for its first Israeli retail store|url=https://www.cultofmac.com/616214/apple-drops-plans-for-its-first-israeli-retail-store/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Cult of Mac|language=en-us}}</ref> and RealFace<ref>{{Cite web|title=Apple's Silicon Chief Talks About iPhone Chips, Face ID, and More in Israeli Interview [Updated]|url=https://www.macrumors.com/2017/11/14/johny-srouji-talks-israel-face-id-and-more/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=MacRumors|date=14 November 2017 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Apple reportedly acquires Israeli AI startup RealFace|url=https://tech.eu/brief/apple-buys-realface/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Tech.eu|date=19 February 2017|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Solomon|first=Shoshanna|title=Apple buys Israel's facial recognition firm RealFace – report|url=http://www.timesofisrael.com/apple-buys-israels-facial-recognition-firm-realface-report/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=[[The Times of Israel]]|date=19 February 2017 |language=en-US |issn=0040-7909}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Apple has acquired more AI startups than Google, Facebook or any other Big Tech company. Is Apple bulking up in AI or just catching up?|url=https://www.businessinsider.in/tech/news/apple-leads-other-tech-giants-in-ai-purchases-which-could-boost-its-shift-toward-digital-services-analysis-finds/articleshow/77159822.cms|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Business Insider}}</ref>
* [[USB flash drive]] – a [[flash memory]] [[data storage device]] integrated with a [[USB]] interface.  The Israeli company [[M-Systems]] (in partnership with IBM) claims to have developed and [[original equipment manufacturer|manufactured]] the first USB flash drives available in North America.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.time.com/time/specials/packages/article/0,28804,2023689_2023703_2023613,00.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101028085259/http://www.time.com/time/specials/packages/article/0,28804,2023689_2023703_2023613,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=October 28, 2010|title=DiskOnKey - All-TIME 100 Gadgets - TIME|date=25 October 2010|work=TIME.com|access-date=10 July 2015|first=Peter|last=Ha}}</ref> This claim is challenged by multiple companies in the following three [[country|countries]] which also independently developed USB technology: [[Singapore]] ([[Trek Technology]]), the [[History of Science and Technology in China|People's Republic of China (PRC)]] ([[Netac Technology]]) and the [[Taiwan|Republic of China (Taiwan)]]. See [[USB flash drive#Patent controversy|USB Flash drive § Patent controversy]].
* [[Web application firewall|WAF]] - a specific form of [[application firewall]] that filters, monitors, and blocks [[HTTP]] [[Network traffic|traffic]] to and from a [[Web application|web service]]. Developed by Gili Raanan

=== Robotics ===
*[[ReWalk]] a bionic walking assistance system that enables [[paraplegics]] to stand upright, walk and climb stairs.
*Development of robotic guidance system for spine surgery by [[Mazor Robotics]].

== Defense, Aviation and Military ==
*[[Iron Dome]] – a mobile [[air defense system]] in development by [[Rafael Advanced Defense Systems]] and [[Israel Aircraft Industries]] designed to intercept short-range [[rocket]]s and [[artillery shells]]. On April 7, 2011, the system successfully intercepted a [[Grad rocket]] launched from [[Gaza Strip|Gaza]], marking the first time in history a short-range rocket was ever intercepted.<ref name="firstInterceptAshkelon">{{cite news|author=Anshel Pfeffer|author2=Yanir Yagna|date=7 April 2011|title=Iron Dome successfully intercepts Gaza rocket for first time|newspaper=Haaretz|url=https://www.haaretz.com/news/diplomacy-defense/iron-dome-successfully-intercepts-gaza-rocket-for-first-time-1.354696|access-date=7 April 2011}}</ref> The Iron Dome was later utilized more fully in the Israeli-Gaza conflict of 2012, where it displayed a very high rate of efficiency (95%–99%) in intercepting enemy projectiles. The United States has been an essential partner in developing the Iron Dome by providing billions towards its development.<ref>{{cite web|title=U.S. pledges funding for Israel´s Iron Dome air defense system CCTV News - CNTV English|url=http://english.cntv.cn/20120518/101009.shtml|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150711222618/http://english.cntv.cn/20120518/101009.shtml|archive-date=11 July 2015|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
*The [[Uzi submachine gun]] was developed by Maj. [[Uziel Gal]] in the 1950s.
*[[IMI Tavor TAR-21]] is an Israeli [[bullpup]] [[assault rifle]].
*[[IMI Desert Eagle|Desert Eagle]] a large-caliber pistol.
*[[Barrel bomb]] - an improvised [[unguided bomb]]. first used by the [[Israeli Air Force]] during the [[1948 Arab–Israeli War]].
*[[Sponge bomb]] - a specialized device designed to seal the end of a tunnel. Developed by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) to address the [[Palestinian tunnel warfare in the Gaza Strip|use of tunnels by Hamas in Gaza]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Israel's Innovative Solution In War Against Hamas: 'Sponge Bombs' To Seal Off Gaps In Gaza Tunnels. Explained |url=https://menafn.com/1107319985/Israels-Innovative-Solution-In-War-Against-Hamas-Sponge-Bombs-To-Seal-Off-Gaps-In-Gaza-Tunnels-Explained |accessdate=Oct 30, 2023 |website=menafn.com}}</ref><ref name="thedrive">{{Cite web |last=Trevithick |first=Joseph |date=Oct 27, 2023 |title=Does Israel Really Have 'Sponge Bombs' For Sealing Tunnels? |url=https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone/does-israel-really-have-sponge-bombs-for-sealing-tunnels |accessdate=Oct 30, 2023 |website=The Drive}}</ref>
*[[CornerShot]] - a weapon accessory created by Lt. Col. Amos Golan of the [[Israeli Defense Forces]] in cooperation with American investors.
*[[Ukraine Siren Alerts]] - a [[siren alert]] electronic system created by Israeli student [[Bernard Moerdler|Bernard 'Boaz' Moerdler]] in 2022.
*[[Tactical Robotics Cormorant]] - a [[flying car]] [[unmanned aerial vehicle]] (UAV) built by Tactical Robotics Ltd. Invented by [[Rafi Yoeli]] in [[Yavne]], Israel.<ref name="iHLS20131128">{{cite news |author=<!-- Unstated --> |date=November 28, 2013 |title=Global Executives Toured the Israeli Defense Industries |url=http://i-hls.com/2013/11/global-executives-toured-the-israeli-defense-industries/ |website=Israel's Homeland Security (iHLS)}}</ref><ref name="Haaretz20110204">{{Cite news |last=Grimland |first=Guy |date=February 4, 2011 |title=When cars fly |url=https://www.haaretz.com/weekend/week-s-end/when-cars-fly-1.341214 |work=Haaretz}}</ref>
* Wall radar – a unique radar utilizing Ultra Wide Band (UWB) to allow users to see through walls. Developed by the Israeli company Camro.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.haaretz.com/news/israeli-company-develops-radar-system-that-sees-through-walls-1.247540|title=Israeli company develops radar system that sees through walls|author=Guy Grimland|date=11 June 2008|work=Haaretz.com|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
*[[Protector USV]] is an [[unmanned surface vehicle]], developed by the [[Rafael Advanced Defense Systems]]. It is the first of its kind to be used in combat.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Protector |work=Defence Updates |url=http://www.defense-update.com/products/p/protector.htm |access-date=2007-04-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070503115143/http://www.defense-update.com/products/p/protector.htm |archive-date=2007-05-03 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
*[[Trophy APS]] - an [[Active Protection System]] designed to protect vehicles from incoming threats, such as [[Rocket-propelled grenade]] rounds and [[ATGMs]] by firing charges to intercept them after detecting and tracking them.<ref>{{Cite web | title=Archived copy | url=https://www.rafael.co.il/system/trophy-aps/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240618040139/https://www.rafael.co.il/system/trophy-aps/ | archive-date=2024-06-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web | title=Archived copy | url=https://www.rafael.co.il/blog/trophy-aps/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241003223405/https://www.rafael.co.il/blog/trophy-aps/ | archive-date=2024-10-03}}</ref>

== Agriculture ==
* Plastic emitter for practical surface [[drip irrigation]] - developed in Israel by [[Simcha Blass]] and his son Yeshayahu in 1959. Rather than allowing water to flow through small holes that could easily be clogged by fine particles, this method directed it through wider and longer channels, using friction within a plastic emitter to slow the flow. The technology is widely used in arid and semi-arid areas all over the world.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Goyal |first=Megh R. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c8TFYZd9cw4C&pg=PA104 |title=Management of Drip/Trickle or Micro Irrigation |date=2012-07-19 |publisher=CRC Press |isbn=978-1-926895-12-3 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Goudie |first=Andrew S. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5YrqQqW11aYC&pg=RA1-PA502 |title=Encyclopedia of Global Change: Environmental Change and Human Society 2 Volumes |date=2002 |publisher=Oxford University Press, USA |isbn=978-0-19-510825-5 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Postel |first=Sandra |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_q4JBAAAQBAJ&pg=PA103 |title=The Last Oasis: Facing Water Scarcity |date=2014-07-16 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-134-16158-4 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Behnke |first=Roy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=dEM0J1Iyhy0C&pg=PA213 |title=The Socio-Economic Causes and Consequences of Desertification in Central Asia |date=2008-07-24 |publisher=Springer Science & Business Media |isbn=978-1-4020-8544-4 |language=en}}</ref>
* The ''[[Tomaccio]]'' cherry tomato was developed by several Israeli laboratories, the dominant ones being those led by Professor [[Nahum Keidar]] and Professor {{Ill|Chaim Rabinovitch|he|חיים רבינוביץ (פרופסור)}} from the Agriculture Faculty of the [[Hebrew University of Jerusalem]], Rehovot Campus.<ref>{{cite web|title=Tomato 'Tomaccio'|url=http://www.raker.com/|access-date=24 October 2014|publisher=Raker}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Tomaccio Sweet Raisin Tomatoes|url=http://www.tomaccio.com/|access-date=2014-08-22|publisher=Tomaccio.com}}</ref>
* The Israeli GFA company which allows fish to be raised virtually anywhere by eliminating the environmental problems in conventional [[fish farming]], without being dependent on electricity or proximity to a body of water.<ref name=israel-feeds-world/>
* Mirtra – a Tal-Ya Water Technologies agricultural technology invention. A unique, patented polypropelyne Mitra system that covers the plant's root system, directing water and fertilizer directly to the root, while protecting the earth around the root from weeds and extreme temperatures, reducing the need to water crops by up to 50 percent. It also reduces fertilizer needs by 50% and functions as an alternative to herbicide (weed-killer).<ref name=israel-feeds-world/><ref>{{Cite web|title=Tal-Ya|url=http://www.tal-ya.com/|access-date=2021-06-02|website=www.tal-ya.com}}</ref> 
* Hybrid cucumber seeds – In the 1950s, Prof. Esra Galun of the [[Weizmann Institute]] developed hybrid seed production of cucumbers and melons, disease-resistant cucumbers and cucumbers suitable for mechanical harvesting. Galun and his colleagues invented a technique for producing hybrid cucumber seeds without hand pollination.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://wis-wander.weizmann.ac.il/hybrid-seed-production#.UWvZJMrTc78|title=Hybrid Seed Production|access-date=10 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150712111403/http://wis-wander.weizmann.ac.il/hybrid-seed-production#.UWvZJMrTc78|archive-date=12 July 2015|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

== Energy ==
* [[Super iron battery]] – A new class of a rechargeable electric battery based on a special kind of iron. More environment friendly because the super-iron eventually rusts, it was developed by [[Stuart Licht]].<ref>{{cite journal | doi = 10.1039/b400251b|title= rechargeable Fe(III/VI) super-iron cathodes | journal=Chemical Communications |issue= 6 | pages=628–629|year= 2004 |last1= Licht |first1= Stuart |last2= Tel-Vered |first2= Ran |pmid= 15010752 }}</ref> of the University of Massachusetts.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.factsofisrael.com/blog/archives/000463.html|title=FactsOfIsrael.com: Hydrogen fuel-cell cars developed by Israeli researchers|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>

== Consumer goods and appliances ==
[[File:Epileertoestel.JPG|180px|right|thumb|[[Epilator]]]]
*[[Wonder Pot]] – a pot developed for baking on the stovetop rather than in an oven.<ref>"Roast in the Wonder Pot", ''The Kosher For Pesach Cookbook'' (1978). Jerusalem:Yeshivat Aish HaTorah Women's Organization, p. 58.</ref>

== Games ==
[[File:Rummikub1.jpg|thumb|upright|[[Rummikub]]]]
* [[Rummikub]] – a [[tile-based game]] for two to four players invented by [[Ephraim Hertzano]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rummy-games.com/rules/rummikub.html|title=Rummikub® Rules - Rules To Rummy Games|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skooldays.com/categories/toys/ty1238.htm|title=Rummikub|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[Mastermind (board game)|Mastermind]] – an Israeli [[board game]] invented by [[Mordecai Meirowitz]].
* [[Guess Who?]] – a two-player guessing game invented by Theo & Ora Coster (a.k.a. Theora Design).
* [[Hidato]] – a [[logic]] [[puzzle]] game invented by mathematician [[Gyora Benedek]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.amuniversal.com/ups/features/hidato/bio.htm|title=Universal Uclick - Home|access-date=10 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111219021011/http://www.amuniversal.com/ups/features/hidato/bio.htm|archive-date=19 December 2011|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>
* [[Taki (card game)|Taki]] – an Israeli [[card game]] invented by {{Ill|Haim Shafir|he|חיים שפיר}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://boredgamegeeks.blogspot.com/2005/09/meet-haim-shafir.html|title=Gone Gaming|author=Yehuda Berlinger|date=6 September 2005|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
* [[Clawee]] - a claw machine game, played online on real [[Arcade game|arcade machines]], invented by the [[Israel|Israeli]] company Gigantic.<ref name="Hacohen Hagai">Hacohen, Hagai (April 20, 2021). [https://www.jpost.com/jerusalem-report/clawee-the-arcade-machines-in-israel-you-can-play-anywhere-in-the-world-665738 Clawee: The arcade machines in Israel you can play anywhere in the world]. [[Jerusalem Post]].</ref>
* [[Pop it|Go Pop]] (today named Pop it) - popular [[fidget toy]] consisting of a usually-brightly colored silicone tray with poppable bubbles, similar to [[bubble wrap]], that can be flipped and re-used. invented in 1975 by [[Ora and Theo Coster|Theo and Ora Coster]].

== Food and drink ==
[[File:Shkedeymarak.jpg|thumb|upright|''Shkedei marak'' (croutons)]]
* [[Shkedei marak]] is an [[Israeli cuisine|Israeli]] food product consisting of crisp mini [[croutons]] used as a soup accompaniment.<ref>[https://books.google.com/books?id=dWZ5CgAAQBAJ&dq=shkedei+marak&pg=PA182 Falafel Nation: Cuisine and the Making of National Identity in Israel, Yael Raviv]</ref> 
* [[Bamba (snack)|Bamba]] is a peanut butter-flavored snack food manufactured by the [[Osem (company)|Osem]] corporation in [[Holon]], Israel.<ref>[http://content.time.com/time/health/article/0,8599,1968474,00.html Time (magazine)]</ref> In a clinical trial published in the [[New England Journal of Medicine]], infants exposed to Bamba had an overall 86% reduction in the development of a peanut allergy, measured at age 5.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Toit |first1=George Du |last2=Roberts |first2=Graham |last3=Sayre |first3=Peter H. |last4=Bahnson |first4=Henry T. |last5=Radulovic |first5=Suzana |last6=Santos |first6=Alexandra F. |last7=Brough |first7=Helen A. |last8=Phippard |first8=Deborah |last9=Basting |first9=Monica |last10=Feeney |first10=Mary |last11=Turcanu |first11=Victor |last12=Sever |first12=Michelle L. |last13=Lorenzo |first13=Margarita Gomez |last14=Plaut |first14=Marshall |last15=Lack |first15=Gideon |date=2015-02-02 |title=Randomized Trial of Peanut Consumption in Infants at Risk for Peanut Allergy |journal=The New England Journal of Medicine |language=en |volume=372 |issue=9 |pages=803–813 |doi=10.1056/NEJMoa1414850 |pmid=25705822|doi-access=free |pmc=4416404 }}</ref> 
* [[Bissli]] is an Israeli wheat snack produced by Nestle-owned [[Osem (company)|Osem]]. Bissli is Osem's leading snack brand after [[Bamba (snack)|Bamba]].<ref name="OSEM">[http://www.osem.co.il/en/brands/snacks Osem: Bissli] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120627050447/http://www.osem.co.il/en/brands/snacks |date=June 27, 2012 }}</ref>
* [[Jerusalem mixed grill]] is a grilled meat dish considered a specialty of Jerusalem. It consists of chicken hearts, spleens and livers mixed with bits of lamb cooked on a flat grill, seasoned with onion, garlic, juniper berries, black pepper, cumin, turmeric and coriander
* [[Sabich]] is a sandwich, consisting of a pita stuffed with fried eggplant and hard-cooked eggs. Local consumption is said to have stemmed from a tradition among Iraqi Jews, who ate it on Shabbat morning.
* [[Ptitim]] - also called Israeli couscous worldwide, is a wheat-based baked pasta. It was initially invented during the [[Austerity in Israel|austerity period in Israel]] when [[rice]] and [[semolina]] were scarce.
* [[Ziva (dish)|Ziva]] - an Israeli dish made of [[puff pastry]] topped with [[Sesame seed|sesame seeds]]. invented by Ziva Twill in 1989
* Shoogi - a type of Israeli [[energy bar]], sometimes consisting of [[Breakfast cereal|cereal]] and comes in different flavours. Invented by [[Talma (company)|Talma]] in 1989.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Anat |first=Bein |title=תלמה מכניסה את "שוגי" לשוק חטיפי הדגנים |url=https://www.themarker.com/advertising/2002-04-08/ty-article/0000017f-f0c4-d8a1-a5ff-f0ceea3a0000 |website=The Marker}}</ref>
* [[Kristal (drink)|Kristal]] - A series of soda and non-soda drinks made by the Israeli Yapaura-Taburi company since 1958.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sela |first=David |title=השבוע לפני 70 שנה: עידן סילון בחיל האוויר |url=https://www.israelhayom.co.il/magazine/shishabat/article/14357869 |access-date= |website=[[Israel Hayom]]|date=8 March 2025 }}</ref> 
* Shalva - a type of a [[snack]] made from [[Puffed grain|puffed wheat]]. Made in Holon, Israel, since the late 1960s.
* [[Limonana]] - a variation of [[mint lemonade]]. a [[portmanteau]] of ''limon'' ({{langx|he|לימון}}) 'lemon' and ''naʿnaʿ'' ({{langx|he|נענע}}) 'mint'.<ref>{{cite web |title=Limonana: Summer Drinks |url=https://www.hadassahmagazine.org/2019/07/16/frozen-coffee-limonana-drinks-summer/ |access-date=17 March 2020 |website=Hadassah Magazine|date=16 July 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite book |last=Lewis |first=Dan |title=Now I Know: The Revealing Stories Behind the World's Most Interesting Facts |publisher=Simon and Schuster |year=2013 |isbn=9781440563638 |chapter=Limonana - when life gives you advertising space, make lemonade |chapter-url=https://books.google.com/books?id=8mXrDQAAQBAJ&q=limonana&pg=PT273}}</ref>
* Tropit - A fruit-flavored drink produced by the Israeli company Yapaura-Taburi.

== Physical exercise ==
* [[Kapap]] (''lit.'' face-to-face combat) - a type of face-to-face combat system. The system was developed in the late 1930s, within the Jewish Aliyah camps as part of preparatory training before their arrival in [[Mandatory Palestine]].
* [[Feldenkrais]] - a type of [[Exercise therapy|movement therapy]] devised by Israeli [[Moshé Feldenkrais]] from 1950 to 1984.<ref name="ehm">{{cite book |url=https://archive.org/details/examiningholisti0000unse_w1i5 |title=Examining Holistic Medicine |publisher=Prometheus Books |year=1989 |isbn=9780879755539 |veditors=Stalker D, Glymour C |page=[https://archive.org/details/examiningholisti0000unse_w1i5/page/373 373] |quote=a system of exercise therapy developed in the 1940s by former judo instructor Moshe Feldenkrais |url-access=registration}}</ref><ref name="Corgi2">{{cite book |last1=Singh |first1=Simon |author-link=Simon Singh |title=Trick or Treatment? Alternative Medicine on Trial |last2=Ernst |first2=Edzard |author-link2=Edzard Ernst |publisher=Bantam Press |year=2008 |isbn=978-0-59306-129-9 |pages=314}}</ref><ref name="Ben Gurion's Personal Trainer">{{cite news |last1=Lori |first1=Aviva |title=Ben Gurion's Personal Trainer |url=https://www.haaretz.com/ben-gurion-s-personal-trainer-1.124885 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160503132051/http://www.haaretz.com/ben-gurion-s-personal-trainer-1.124885 |archive-date=3 May 2016 |access-date=4 June 2016 |newspaper=Haaretz}}</ref>
* [[Krav Maga]] - an Israeli [[Hand-to-hand combat|self defence system]] and [[martial art]], developed by [[Imi Lichtenfeld]] from 1948 to 1998.<ref>{{cite web |last=Korvo |first=Kevin |date=2008-09-30 |title=Krav Maga: training for self-defense and fitness |url=http://www.snponline.com/articles/2008/09/30/multiple_papers/business/allhlkravm_20080930_1144am_3.txt |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151105192913/http://www.snponline.com/articles/2008/09/30/multiple_papers/business/allhlkravm_20080930_1144am_3.txt |archive-date=2015-11-05 |access-date=2008-10-11 |publisher=[[Mansfield News Journal]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=2005-10-22 |title=The mother of all fightbacks |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/education/3352222/The-mother-of-all-fightbacks.html |access-date=2010-01-01 |work=[[Daily Telegraph]] |location=London}}</ref>

== Other ==
* [[Dog TV|DogTV]] - The first dedicated television network designed for dogs. Created by Israeli Ron Lev and originally launched in Israel.<ref>{{Cite web|title=DOGTV|url=https://finder.startupnationcentral.org/company_page/dogtv|access-date=2021-06-02|website=Start-Up Nation Finder}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2015-04-14|title=Don't press paws: how DogTV built a global TV channel for man's best friends|url=http://www.theguardian.com/technology/2015/apr/14/dogtv-global-tv-channel-dogs-no-cats|access-date=2021-06-02|website=the Guardian|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mfa.gov.il/MFA/IsraelExperience/Pages/DogTV-2-Dec-2012.aspx|access-date=2021-06-02|website=mfa.gov.il}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|title=Pass the remote, Rex: Meet the founder of DOGTV|url=https://www.haaretz.com/.premium-pass-the-remote-rex-meet-dogtv-founder-1.5225056|access-date=2021-06-02|newspaper=Haaretz|language=en}}</ref> [https://www.linkedin.com/in/yossi-uzrad-819711a/]<ref>https://www.linkedin.com/in/leviron/ {{Self-published source|date=June 2022}}</ref>
* [[Gaga (dance vocabulary)|Gaga (dance vocabulary) -]] '''Gaga''' is a movement language and [[pedagogy]] developed by [[Batsheva Dance Company]] director and teacher [[Ohad Naharin]]. Used in some Israeli contemporary dance, it has two educational tracks which are taught in Israel as well as several other countries
*[[Epilator]] (originally "Epilady") –  a feminine beauty product. It was developed and originally manufactured at Kibbutz [[HaGoshrim]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mako.co.il/women-magazine/diva/Article-f59708410f7e021006.htm|title=גאווה נשית: המצאות ישראליות שעושות לנו כבוד|work=mako|date=28 April 2009|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.ynet.co.il/articles/0,7340,L-2621280,00.html|title=ynet המצאות ישראליות ששיגעו את העולם - דיגיטל|work=ynet|access-date=10 July 2015}}</ref>
